summary: Tested LandIt Pro features (web app at /app/web): desktop layout, Vercel Analytics events, Stripe Checkout. Backend pytest 10/11 passed. Web frontend renders all 5 routes (home/analyze/pro/history/about), responsive layout works (mobile bottom-tabs at 390px, desktop top-nav-pill + 2-col textarea grid at 1280px), Pro localStorage hides quota and shows PRO crown badge, About->Pro navigation works, GO PRO button calls /api/checkout and gets a 200. TWO blocking issues found: (1) EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env is INVALID — every /api/analyze call returns 502 'OpenAIException - Invalid API key'; (2) /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 502 due to Pydantic validation error in emergentintegrations Stripe library (metadata field gets StripeObject, expects dict) — this BREAKS the /pro/success polling flow.

critical.issue: Returns 502 'litellm.AuthenticationError: OpenAIException - Invalid API key'. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=sk-emergent-972Eb1b78B9949eB48 in /app/backend/.env appears truncated/rotated. Worked in iteration_1 but is now broken. Replace key.

Returns 502 'Stripe error: Unexpected error retrieving session status: 1 validation error for CheckoutStatusResponse metadata Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject ...]'. The emergentintegrations Stripe wrapper returns metadata as StripeObject, but its own pydantic CheckoutStatusResponse expects dict. This BREAKS the /pro/success page which polls this endpoint to confirm payment & flip is_pro=true. Reproducible: POST /api/checkout to create a session, then GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} → 502.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking ANALYZE renders red error banner: 'AI provider error: ... Invalid API key'. Caused by backend EMERGENT_LLM_KEY issue (see backend critical #1). Frontend correctly surfaces the error.

ProSuccess page will fail when it polls /api/checkout/status/{session_id} (returns 502). Even after a real Stripe payment, the device will not flip to Pro via the polling path. Webhook path may still work, but redirect-back UX will show an error.

action_items: Replace EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env with a valid key (current value sk-emergent-972Eb1b78B9949eB48 is rejected by litellm/OpenAI proxy). Restart backend.

Fix /api/checkout/status pydantic validation error from emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout. Options: (a) bump/pin emergentintegrations version, (b) wrap stripe.get_checkout_status in a try/except that falls back to reading payment_transactions row + raw stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve via stripe SDK to get status/payment_status without metadata, or (c) call the Stripe SDK directly instead of through emergentintegrations for the status read.
After fixes, retest: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_landit_pro.py + /app/backend/tests/test_analyze.py and the Analyze + Pro flows in /app/web.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py logger is initialized AFTER it is referenced by exception handlers in /api/analyze and /api/checkout/status. At runtime this works because logging.basicConfig runs at import time before requests, but it is fragile — move basicConfig + logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) to the top of the file.
/api/checkout success path: amount/currency/package label are correctly server-defined (PRO_PACKAGES constant); frontend amount is never trusted. Good.
payment_transactions inserts use server-defined amount; status is updated atomically via {'fulfilled': {'$ne': True}} guard; double-fulfillment safe. Good.
/api/checkout validates origin_url scheme (http/https) and device_id length (<=64). Good.
All MongoDB reads use {'_id': 0} projection. No _id leakage observed in any tested response.

success_rate.backend: 10/11 = 91% (root, analyze-too-short, checkout valid, checkout 4x invalid 400s, checkout/status 404, webhook route, pro-status unknown all PASS; checkout/status existing session FAIL=502 pydantic)
success_rate.frontend: 11/13 = ~85% (all 5 screens render, responsive desktop @1280 verified 2-col + top-nav-pill, mobile bottom-tabs, pro-pill via localStorage, about->pro nav, checkout API call returns 200 with stripe.com URL [confirmed via pytest]; FAIL: analyze flow blocked by backend LLM key, pro-success polling will fail)

seed_data_creation: test_landit_pro.py creates one Stripe checkout session with device_id='TEST_dev_pytest_001' (test-mode, never paid; will expire on Stripe side). One payment_transactions doc inserted in mongo with payment_status='initiated'. Safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vite preview already running at :4173 (yarn preview --host 0.0.0.0 --port 4173). Public web URL goes through EXPO_BACKEND_URL = https://job-match-scanner.preview.emergentagent.com. To retest after fixes: (1) curl /api/analyze with a >=30-char JD — must return 200; (2) pytest /app/backend/tests/test_landit_pro.py — all 11 should pass; (3) load http://localhost:4173/analyze, paste JD+resume, click analyze-btn, expect analyze-result-screen; (4) ProSuccess: open /pro/success?session_id=<existing> and verify no 502 error. Stripe is in TEST mode — DO NOT complete a real payment.
rca_of_issue: Issue 1 (LLM key): EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=sk-emergent-972Eb1b78B9949eB48 in /app/backend/.env. The proxy at integrations.emergentagent.com rejects this key with OpenAI 401. Iteration 1 (a few hours ago) saw this key work; key has been rotated/expired. Reproduction: `curl -s -X POST $EXPO_BACKEND_URL/api/analyze -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"job_description":"...30+ chars...","resume":""}' → 502`. Mitigation: set a valid key and `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`. Issue 2 (checkout/status 502): emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status builds a CheckoutStatusResponse(BaseModel) where `metadata: dict`, but stripe-python returns metadata as a StripeObject (a dict subclass) — pydantic v2 strict mode rejects it. Reproduction: POST /api/checkout then GET /api/checkout/status/{returned_session_id} → 502 with the exact validation traceback in the response detail. Mitigation: pin/upgrade emergentintegrations OR bypass it by importing `stripe` directly: `stripe.api_key = STRIPE_API_KEY; sess = stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id)` and reading `sess.status` and `sess.payment_status` — no metadata coercion needed for this read.
